sse.description: Server-Sent Events fallback for proxies or environments that strip the WebSocket Upgrade header. Server-to-client only: identical envelope shape, no client subscription control frames or WHEP signaling.
sse.title: SSE realtime channel (fallback)

Sec-WebSocket-Protocol.description: Subprotocol selection; makes the envelope major explicit.

query.description: Optional authentication token as a query parameter.

ws.description: Bidirectional WebSocket channel. The server sends event envelopes; the client sends subscription control frames ($subscribe, $unsubscribe, $set_rate, $resume, $pong). Negotiated subprotocol: `multiview.v1` (or `multiview.bin.v1` for compact binary meter frames).
ws.title: WebSocket realtime channel

AlarmTransition.name: AlarmTransition
AlarmTransition.summary: An alarm was raised, updated, cleared, or acknowledged (topic `alarms`).
AlarmTransition.title: Monitoring alarm lifecycle event

Alert.description: The `key` field is a stable dedupe identifier for the condition; multiple `alert.raised` frames with the same key coalesce rather than stacking.
Alert.name: Alert
Alert.summary: An alert was raised (alert.raised) or cleared (alert.cleared) on topic `alerts`.
Alert.title: Operator alert raised or cleared

AudioLoudness.description: Emitted on topic `audio.loudness` (AUD-8). A high-rate CONFLATED compliance lane (BS.1770-4 / EBU Tech 3341/3342, ADR-R006): the slow-cadence M/S/I/LRA/dBTP sample the loudness meter measures read-only off the audio bus, pushed at ~10 Hz and excluded from the lossless replay ring. A slow consumer can only lose its own samples — the engine never back-pressures (inv #10). BALLISTICS are applied client-side: the wire carries raw measured values; the browser meter applies the display decay/peak-hold. The compliance reference (`target_lufs`/`ceiling_dbtp`/`tolerance_lu`) rides every sample so the meter colours against the same target the program bus is normalised toward.
AudioLoudness.name: AudioLoudness
AudioLoudness.summary: Momentary/short-term/integrated LUFS + LRA + true-peak (dBTP) + compliance reference (numeric only).
AudioLoudness.title: Program-bus EBU R128 loudness sample

AudioMeter.description: Emitted on topic `audio.meters`. This is the sole high-rate conflated topic; frames are conflated/sampled at 10–30 Hz on the wire. A slow consumer can only lose its own meter frames — the engine never back-pressures. The binary fast-path (subprotocol `multiview.bin.v1`) carries the same fields in compact form; this schema describes the decoded (JSON) shape.
AudioMeter.name: AudioMeter
AudioMeter.summary: Per-input/track peak/RMS/clip meter sample (numeric only, never audio data).
AudioMeter.title: High-rate audio meter sample

CastSessionRemoved.description: Emitted on topic `devices` with envelope `id` = session id when a session is stopped (`DELETE /api/v1/cast/sessions/{id}` — the receiver STOP that clears the TV) or promoted to a saved device (`POST /{id}/save` retires the ephemeral record while playback continues under the promoted device id).
CastSessionRemoved.name: CastSessionRemoved
CastSessionRemoved.summary: An ad-hoc cast session was removed (cast.session.removed on topic `devices`; lossless lifecycle lane).
CastSessionRemoved.title: Ephemeral cast session removed

CastSessionStarted.description: Emitted on topic `devices` with envelope `id` = session id when `POST /api/v1/cast/sessions` accepts a start (the runtime record exists and its supervised actor is spawned): the session-list MEMBERSHIP changed, so clients refresh the list immediately instead of waiting for a REST re-poll. The session's live state rides the conflated `device.status` lane keyed by the same session id.
CastSessionStarted.name: CastSessionStarted
CastSessionStarted.summary: An ad-hoc cast session was started (cast.session.started on topic `devices`; lossless lifecycle lane).
CastSessionStarted.title: Ephemeral cast session started

DeviceAdopted.name: DeviceAdopted
DeviceAdopted.summary: A device was adopted (device.adopted on topic `devices`; lossless lifecycle lane).
DeviceAdopted.title: Device adopted into the registry

DeviceDiscovered.description: Discovery results are an UNTRUSTED inventory requiring explicit confirm-adopt — never auto-ingested. IPv6-first: IPv4 results are explicitly labelled `ipv4-legacy`.
DeviceDiscovered.name: DeviceDiscovered
DeviceDiscovered.summary: A discovery row streamed while a scan operation runs (device.discovered on topic `devices`, correlated via `corr`).
DeviceDiscovered.title: Untrusted discovery-inventory row

DeviceError.name: DeviceError
DeviceError.summary: A device error was reported (device.error on topic `devices`; lossless lifecycle lane).
DeviceError.title: Driver-reported device error

DeviceMode.description: Carries the impact declared BEFORE apply per the instant-apply doctrine: vendor-decoder mode convergence is `dev`-class (the device pipeline restarts; Multiview program output is unaffected).
DeviceMode.name: DeviceMode
DeviceMode.summary: Mode convergence started/finished/failed with its declared impact (device.mode on topic `devices`).
DeviceMode.title: Device mode convergence

DeviceRemoved.name: DeviceRemoved
DeviceRemoved.summary: A device was removed (device.removed on topic `devices`; lossless lifecycle lane).
DeviceRemoved.title: Device removed from the registry

DeviceStatus.description: Emitted on topic `devices` with envelope `id` = device id. CONFLATED latest-wins telemetry (vendor-polled at ~1 s cadence): the latest value supersedes all prior values, and the event is excluded from the lossless replay ring — a re-snapshot heals it (ADR-RT007 extends ADR-RT003's ring-exclusion rule to per-event-type granularity on this mixed-cadence topic). Staleness is surfaced via `last_seen_ts`, never papered over.
DeviceStatus.name: DeviceStatus
DeviceStatus.title: Conflated per-device runtime snapshot

DeviceSync.name: DeviceSync
DeviceSync.summary: Sync-group membership / achieved-tier change or drift-threshold crossing (device.sync on topic `devices`).
DeviceSync.title: Device sync participation change

Envelope.description: Every message in both directions uses this envelope. The `t` field is the discriminator selecting the `data` schema. The `seq` field is a per-connection monotonic cursor; gaps indicate dropped frames and trigger resume/resync. The `ts` field carries engine-monotonic nanoseconds (same clock family as output PTS). `v` is the envelope schema major; clients MUST reject unknown majors.
Envelope.name: Envelope

corr.description: Optional correlation id echoing a REST command / job.

data.description: Typed payload selected by `t`. Schema depends on the event type.

seq.description: Per-connection monotonic resume cursor. A gap means frames were dropped.

t.description: Dotted event type; the discriminator selecting the `data` schema.

topic.description: Subscription routing key. Control frames use `$control`.

ts.description: Engine monotonic nanoseconds (same clock family as output PTS).

v.description: Envelope schema major. A receiver MUST reject an unknown major.

Envelope.summary: The single wire frame used for all realtime messages (ADR-RT002).
Envelope.title: Versioned realtime envelope

HealthWarning.description: A richer sibling of `Alert` carrying a stable `code`, the affected `subsystem`, and a concrete `remediation`. The `code` is the dedupe key: frames with the same code coalesce rather than stacking. Capability-mismatch codes (e.g. `gpu-present-no-vulkan-adapter`) are latched build-time facts — raised once, cleared on reconfigure/restart.
HealthWarning.name: HealthWarning
HealthWarning.summary: A health warning was raised (health.warning.raised) or cleared (health.warning.cleared) on topic `alerts`.
HealthWarning.title: Actionable health warning raised or cleared

Hello.name: Hello
Hello.summary: First server frame after auth; advertises connection parameters.
Hello.title: $hello control frame

InputConnection.name: InputConnection
InputConnection.summary: An input source transitioned connection state (input.connection on topic `inputs`).
InputConnection.title: Input source connection state change

InputStreams.description: Read-only discovery: every elementary stream (video / audio tracks / subtitles / SCTE-35 / KLV / timecode) the input offers, each with a stable kind-scoped id. Emitted on first appearance and as a delta on re-probe / PMT-version bump.
InputStreams.name: InputStreams
InputStreams.summary: An input's stream inventory appeared or changed on re-probe (input.streams on topic `inputs`).
InputStreams.title: Input elementary-stream inventory

JobProgress.name: JobProgress
JobProgress.summary: Progress of a REST command job correlated by `corr` (topic `jobs`).
JobProgress.title: Long-running job progress

MediaPlayerEvent.description: Emitted on topic `switcher` with envelope `id` = player id. A LOSSLESS lifecycle event kept in the bounded replay ring (ADR-RT008): discrete transport-state changes (cued / playing / paused / stopped / vamping{exit_armed} / eof / loading) plus the playhead `position_frames`. It must never be conflated — a control surface reconstructs authoritative player history from it. Per-frame position is NOT streamed; clients interpolate `position_frames` between events at the output cadence.
MediaPlayerEvent.name: MediaPlayerEvent
MediaPlayerEvent.summary: A media player transitioned transport state (media.player_state on topic `switcher`).
MediaPlayerEvent.title: Media-player transport-state lifecycle

OutputStatus.description: Emitted on topic `outputs`. Make-before-break migration emits `migrating` during the parallel-output window.
OutputStatus.name: OutputStatus
OutputStatus.summary: An output sink changed run state (starting/running/migrating/error).
OutputStatus.title: Output sink status update

SalvoEvent.name: SalvoEvent
SalvoEvent.summary: A salvo was armed, taken, or cancelled (topic `tally`).
SalvoEvent.title: Salvo arm/take lifecycle

ShedLoad.description: A discrete, lossless degradation-signal event (invariant #9): the engine relieved sustained overload by shedding work rather than blocking the output clock (invariants #1 + #10). The `reason` says why a shed was chosen over hold/migrate; `scope` says what was shed (program / a specific input / a shared resource); `level` is the degradation-ladder rung after the shed and `dropped` the cumulative frames/units shed. The consent-independent retention store records these for the §7.2 support bundle (ADR-0052).
ShedLoad.name: ShedLoad
ShedLoad.summary: The engine shed work to relieve sustained overload (shed.load on topic `alerts`).
ShedLoad.title: Resource-adaptive shed-load decision

Subscribe.name: Subscribe
Subscribe.summary: Client requests subscription to one or more topics.
Subscribe.title: $subscribe control frame (client to server)

SystemMetrics.description: Emitted on topic `system`. A high-rate conflated lane like `audio.meters`: samples are latest-only at ~1-2 Hz on the wire and excluded from the lossless replay ring. PUSHED, never polled — a slow UI loses only its own samples and the engine never back-pressures (inv #10). Live values only; historic windows (the data decisions are made from) are a separate cold REST query.
SystemMetrics.name: SystemMetrics
SystemMetrics.summary: CPU / GPU / encoder-decoder utilisation sample (numeric only).
SystemMetrics.title: High-rate whole-system metrics sample

TallyEvent.name: TallyEvent
TallyEvent.summary: Resolved tally state for one tile/element changed (topic `tally`).
TallyEvent.title: Tally lamp/UMD state change

TileState.description: Emitted on topic `tiles` whenever a tile's state machine transitions. Invariant #2: Live → Stale → Reconnecting → NoSignal. The compositor holds the last-good frame while STALE/RECONNECTING; renders a placeholder on NO_SIGNAL.
TileState.name: TileState
TileState.summary: A tile transitioned between lifecycle states (LIVE/STALE/RECONNECTING/NO_SIGNAL).
TileState.title: Tile state machine transition

TilesSnapshot.description: Emitted once at connect (after `$hello`) on topic `tiles` with `t` `$snapshot` (realtime.md §5): the snapshot-then-delta baseline (`snapshot ⊕ ordered deltas = current truth`, ADR-RT003). A receiver MUST treat it as a state REBUILD, never a merge. Despite the `$`-prefixed tag it rides its data topic, not `$control`.
TilesSnapshot.name: TilesSnapshot
TilesSnapshot.summary: Full current per-tile lifecycle state a fresh client rebuilds its tile cache from.
TilesSnapshot.title: Connect-time tiles baseline ($snapshot on topic tiles)

TimingStatus.description: Emitted on topic `devices` with envelope `id` = program or sync-group id (ADR-M010). CONFLATED latest-wins and excluded from the lossless replay ring: the epoch is an exact affine map that stays valid when stale, so a receiver that misses updates keeps the last epoch and free-runs — it degrades, never stalls. The achieved tier is the MEASURED tier, never an aspirational one.
TimingStatus.name: TimingStatus
TimingStatus.summary: The per-program WallClockRef epoch, link offset, clock source/quality, and per-sync-group achieved skew (timing.status on topic `devices`).
TimingStatus.title: Outbound presentation epoch + sync telemetry

AchievedSync.description: The MEASURED sync tier actually achieved (never aspirational): frame-accurate (our nodes), bounded-skew (vendor decoders, ±100–500 ms drift), or none (never part of a synchronized canvas).

AlarmTransition.description: Data body of `alarm.raised`, `alarm.updated`, `alarm.cleared`, `alarm.acked`. Carries the current AlarmRecord value after the transition (X.733-aligned).

record.description: The current alarm record after the transition.

Alert.description: Data body of `alert.raised` and `alert.cleared` events. The `key` field is a stable dedupe identifier: multiple raised frames with the same key coalesce.

active.description: Whether the condition is currently active.

key.description: Stable dedupe key for the condition.

AudioLoudness.description: Data body of the `audio.loudness` event: a program-bus EBU R128 loudness sample (M/S/I LUFS, LRA, true-peak dBTP) plus the compliance reference. Numeric metadata only — never audio content. Conflated/sampled at ~10 Hz on the wire (high-rate lane); ballistics are applied client-side. The integrating fields are absent below the -70 LUFS absolute gate (no fabricated value).

gain_db.description: Makeup gain the loudnorm processor is applying, dB. Absent when no normaliser is engaged.

integrated.description: Integrated (gated) loudness, LUFS. Absent until enough gated audio.

lra.description: Loudness range (EBU Tech 3342), LU. Absent until enough gated audio.

momentary.description: Momentary loudness (400 ms window), LUFS. Absent below the absolute gate.

program.description: Program/bus index this loudness sample is for.

short_term.description: Short-term loudness (3 s window), LUFS. Absent below the absolute gate.

target_lufs.description: Normalisation target loudness, LUFS (compliance reference, e.g. -23 / -16).

tolerance_lu.description: Live convergence tolerance, LU (the in-spec band is target ± tolerance).

true_peak_dbtp.description: Maximum true-peak across channels (4x oversampled), dBTP. Absent when disabled.

AudioMeter.description: Data body of the `audio.meter` event: a high-rate per-input/track peak/RMS/clip sample. This is numeric metadata only — never audio content. Conflated/sampled at 10–30 Hz on the wire (high-rate lane).

clip.description: Whether any channel clipped in this window.

overflow.description: Whether the meter pipeline overflowed (dropped windows).

CastSessionRemoved.description: Data body of `cast.session.removed`: an ephemeral cast session was removed — stopped (the receiver STOP that clears the TV) or promoted to a saved device (playback continues under the promoted device id).

session_id.description: The runtime session id that was removed.

CastSessionStarted.description: Data body of `cast.session.started`: an ephemeral cast session was started (session-list membership changed). The session's live state rides the conflated `device.status` lane keyed by the same session id.

address.description: The device authority dialled (`host[:port]`, IPv6 bracketed).

output.description: The output id whose HLS rendition the session casts.

session_id.description: The runtime session id (`cast-session-…`, UUID-fresh per start).

DeviceAdopted.description: Data body of `device.adopted`: a device was adopted into the registry.

name.description: The operator-facing display name, if set.

DeviceCapabilities.description: Fixed probed capability flags — a driver maps its device into exactly this shape.

decode.description: The device can decode (receive a Multiview output).

display.description: The device drives a physical display.

encode.description: The device can encode (offers streams Multiview ingests as Sources).

firmware_update.description: The device supports managed firmware update.

reboot.description: The device can be rebooted via the management channel.

DeviceDiscovered.description: Data body of `device.discovered`: one untrusted discovery-inventory row streamed while a scan runs (correlated via the envelope `corr`). Requires explicit confirm-adopt; never auto-ingested.

address.description: The management endpoint (URL/host; IPv6 literals bracketed).

driver.description: The candidate driver that recognised the device.

family.description: Address family — IPv6-first; IPv4 results are labelled legacy.

name.description: The advertised device name, if any.

DeviceError.description: Data body of `device.error`: a driver-reported device error.

code.description: Short machine-readable code where the driver has one (vendor codes pass through verbatim).

device_id.description: The registry device id the error concerns.

DeviceMode.description: Data body of `device.mode`: a mode convergence started/finished/failed, carrying the impact declared BEFORE apply (instant-apply doctrine).

detail.description: The human-readable declared-impact statement, if the driver provides one.

mode.description: The target mode being converged to (driver vocabulary).

phase.description: Which convergence phase this event reports.

DeviceRemoved.description: Data body of `device.removed`: a device was removed from the registry.

device_id.description: The registry device id that was removed.

DeviceState.description: Managed-device lifecycle state (managed-devices.md §2.2), uppercase on the wire.

DeviceStatus.description: Data body of `device.status` (managed-devices.md §2.1): the conflated latest-wins per-device runtime snapshot, envelope `id` = device id. Excluded from the lossless replay ring (a re-snapshot heals it); staleness is surfaced via `last_seen_ts`.

device_id.description: The registry device id this snapshot describes.

last_seen_ts.description: When the device last answered (engine monotonic nanoseconds).

mode.description: The device's current converged mode (driver vocabulary), once known.

streams.description: Device-reported active streams (omitted when none / unknown).

DeviceStreamStatus.description: One device-reported active stream. Optional fields are absent where the vendor does not report that figure.

healthy.description: Whether the device reports this stream as healthy.

output_ref.description: The Multiview output this decode stream is bound to, if any.

role.description: Whether the device is encoding or decoding this stream.

DeviceSync.description: Data body of `device.sync`: sync-group membership / achieved-tier / drift change.

DeviceSyncSummary.description: A device's sync-group membership summary inside a device.status snapshot.

group.description: The sync group this device belongs to.

offset_ms.description: Per-member presentation offset trim (ms, AES67 link-offset semantics).

GpuMetrics.description: A per-GPU utilisation sample. Optional fields are absent where the vendor does not expose that signal.

encoder_sessions.description: DEVICE-WIDE active concurrent encode sessions (NVIDIA) across all processes.

id.description: Stable device identity (UUID where available, else an index).

self_compute_util.description: Our process's compute (SM) utilisation on this GPU, 0.0-1.0.

self_encoder_sessions.description: Encode sessions owned by our process on this GPU.

self_mem_used_bytes.description: VRAM (bytes) attributed to our process on this GPU.

GpuVendor.description: GPU/accelerator hardware vendor (selects which per-engine signals to expect).

HealthWarning.description: Data body of `health.warning.raised` and `health.warning.cleared`. A richer sibling of `Alert`: the `code` is the stable dedupe key, and `remediation` carries the concrete fix. Capability-mismatch codes are latched build-time facts (raised once, cleared on reconfigure/restart).

active.description: Whether the condition is currently active (raise vs clear).

message.description: A clear, human-readable description of the condition.

remediation.description: The concrete remediation — what the operator must do to fix it.

since.description: When the condition was first raised (engine monotonic nanoseconds).

Hello.description: Data body of `$hello`: the first server frame after auth.

default_rate_hz.description: Default wire cadence when `rate_hz` is omitted from `$subscribe`.

server_v.description: Envelope schema majors this server can speak.

ImpactClass.description: Declared impact class of a management change: cp (control-plane only), c1 (hot/seamless at a frame boundary), c2 (controlled reset via make-before-break), dev (the DEVICE pipeline restarts; Multiview program output is unaffected).

InputStreams.description: Data body of the `input.streams` event: an input's full elementary-stream inventory (RT-3).

input_id.description: The owning input's configured source id.

inventory.description: The StreamInventory (multiview_core::stream::StreamInventory): every elementary stream the input offers.

input_id.description: The input id the inventory is bound to, if known.

streams.description: Every elementary stream, in container order.

JobProgress.description: Data body of `job.progress`; correlated to a REST command via the envelope `corr`.

Lag.description: Data body of `$lag`: this connection's queue overflowed for a topic.

LagAction.description: What the server did with the dropped frames.

LifecycleState.description: Tile/input lifecycle state (invariant #2: Live → Stale → Reconnecting → NoSignal).

MediaPlayerEvent.description: Data body of `media.player_state` (ADR-RT008): a media player's discrete transport-state transition, envelope `id` = player id. LOSSLESS — kept in the replay ring, never conflated. `position_frames` is the playhead in integer frames at the output cadence (never a float; clients interpolate between events).

asset.description: The asset currently loaded in the player, if any.

position_frames.description: Playhead position at the transition, in integer frames at the output cadence.

MediaPlayerState.description: A media player's discrete transport state (tagged by `kind`, never untagged). The `vamping` variant carries `exit_armed`: when set, the current vamp lap finishes then the player exits cleanly at the boundary. `#[non_exhaustive]`: a client must treat an unknown kind as forward-compatible, not an error.

oneOf.description: Opening/decoding the asset toward the cue point; not yet primed.
Asset loaded, in-point frame primed, holding paused — ready to take.
Playing forward at the output cadence.
Looping a fill segment to hold until a cue (the VT vamp/exit feature).

exit_armed.description: Whether a clean exit is armed: finish the current lap, then leave the vamp at the boundary.

oneOf.description: Reached end-of-file with no loop/vamp engaged.

OutputRunState.description: Running state of an output sink.

clients.description: Number of currently-connected consumers, if known.

ProtocolError.description: Data body of `$error`: a control-plane error.

Resume.description: Data body of `$resume` (client→server): present last-seen cursor on reconnect.

Resync.description: Data body of `$resync`: the gap is unrecoverable; the client MUST rebuild state from the fresh snapshot that follows.

head.description: Output head this recall applies to, if scoped.

phase.description: The lifecycle phase this event reports.

SetRate.description: Data body of `$set_rate` (client→server): change a topic's wire cadence.

rate_hz.description: Requested cadence (Hz); server clamps to [min, max].

ShedLoad.description: Data body of the `shed.load` event: a resource-adaptive shed-load decision (invariant #9). The consent-independent retention store records these for the §7.2 support bundle (ADR-0052).

dropped.description: Cumulative frames/units shed under this condition at the time of the event.

level.description: The degradation-ladder level after the shed (0 = full quality).

ShedReason.description: Why the resource-adaptive controller shed load rather than holding or migrating the pipeline (invariant #9). Mirrors the engine's `ShedReason`; additive + non-exhaustive (ADR-RT002/RT003).

ShedScope.description: What a shed-load decision applied to (tagged by `kind`).

oneOf.description: The shed touched the whole-program encode/egress path (a composited frame was dropped).
The shed degraded a specific input/tile (cheapest-impact-first ladder).

id.description: The configured input/source id the shed degraded.

oneOf.description: The shed degraded a shared resource (e.g. a preview/encode pool).

Subscribe.description: Data body of `$subscribe` (client→server): subscribe to topics.

rate_hz.description: Optional max cadence (Hz); server clamps and reports effective.

since_seq.description: Optional resume cursor: subscribe + replay from after this seq.

Subscribed.description: Data body of `$subscribed`: per-topic ack before the snapshot.

snapshot_seq.description: The `seq` the forthcoming snapshot is current as of.

SyncCapability.description: How well a device can participate in synchronized presentation (fixed probed tri-state).

SyncChange.description: What changed about a device's sync participation (tagged by `kind`, never untagged).

exceeded.description: true = crossed above the target; false = recovered back inside it.

measured_skew_ms.description: The measured skew for this member (ms).

SyncGroupSkew.description: One sync group's MEASURED skew/tier (achieved tier = weakest member, never over-claimed).

measured_skew_ms.description: The worst measured member skew (ms), where a measurement exists.

SystemMetrics.description: Data body of the `system.metrics` event: a high-rate whole-system sample (cpu / gpu / encoder-decoder). Numeric only; conflated at ~1-2 Hz (high-rate lane).

gpus.description: Per-GPU utilisation samples; empty on a GPU-free host.

mem_used_bytes.description: Host memory in use (bytes), if known.

program_fps.description: Aggregate program output rate (fps), if running.

TallyEvent.description: Data body of `tally.state`: resolved tally lamp/UMD state for one element.

TallyTarget.description: What a tally state applies to (tagged by `kind`).

TileSnapshotEntry.description: One tile's current lifecycle state inside a `tiles` `$snapshot`. The `id` is the same key the sparse `tile.state` deltas scope their envelope `id` with, so a snapshot-rebuilt cache and its delta patches address the same rows.

id.description: The tile id (the bound source id in the run projection).

input.description: The input bound to the tile, if known.

TileState.description: Data body of the `tile.state` event: a tile lifecycle transition.

input.description: The input bound to the tile at the time of the transition, if any.

TilesSnapshot.description: Data body of the `tiles`-topic `$snapshot` frame: the full current per-tile lifecycle baseline sent once at connect (after `$hello`). A receiver MUST rebuild (replace) its tile state from it, never merge.

as_of_seq.description: The engine state sequence this baseline is current as of.

TimingStatus.description: Data body of `timing.status` (ADR-M010): the outbound presentation epoch plus per-sync-group achieved skew, envelope `id` = program or sync-group id. Latest-wins and ring-excluded: the affine epoch stays valid when stale, so receivers free-run on a missed update — they never stall.

clock_quality.description: The discipline quality of that clock (the engine servo's lock-state lifecycle).

clock_source.description: What disciplines the wall estimate (ST 2059-2 PTP servo or chrony-disciplined system time). The clock labels the timeline; it never paces the tick loop.

groups.description: Per-sync-group measured skew/tier (omitted when no groups exist).

link_offset_ns.description: Fixed receiver-side presentation delay (ns, AES67 link-offset semantics): uniformity is the goal, not smallness.

stream_id.description: The program/output stream this epoch maps.

Unsubscribe.description: Data body of `$unsubscribe` (client→server): stop receiving topics.

WallClockRef.description: The exact affine media↔wall map (multiview_core::wallclock::WallClockRef, ADR-0038): wall(pts) = wall_at_anchor_ns + rescale(pts − media_at_anchor). Integer/rational arithmetic only — never float.

media_at_anchor.description: Media PTS of the anchor sample, in units of `rate`.

rate.description: The media rate (ticks per second) as an exact rational.

wall_at_anchor_ns.description: Wall-clock instant (ns past the Unix epoch) at the anchor sample.

WarningCode.description: Stable catalog code of a health warning (kebab-case). `#[non_exhaustive]`: the catalog grows over time, so a client must treat an unknown code as a forward-compatible warning, not an error.

WarningSeverity.description: Severity of a health warning (sibling of AlertSeverity).

info.description: WebSocket and SSE realtime event stream for the Multiview engine. Every message uses a single versioned envelope (ADR-RT002). Topics: tiles, inputs, outputs, audio.meters, alerts, alarms, tally, layout, config, logs, jobs, preview, system, capabilities, devices, switcher. The WebSocket endpoint (/api/v1/ws) is bidirectional (events + subscription control frames); the SSE endpoint (/api/v1/events) is server-to-client only.
info.title: Multiview Realtime API

subscribe-sse.description: Receive all event types on the SSE fallback channel.
subscribe-sse.title: Receive realtime events (SSE)

subscribe-ws.description: Receive all event types on the WebSocket channel after subscribing to the desired topics via $subscribe control frames.
subscribe-ws.title: Receive realtime events (WebSocket)

sse-server.description: Server-Sent Events endpoint (server→client only). Fallback transport.

host.description: Hostname and optional port of the Multiview daemon. IPv6-first: the default is the IPv6 loopback `[::1]`; override with your host.
Hostname and optional port of the Multiview daemon. IPv6-first: the default is the IPv6 loopback `[::1]`; override with your host.
